articles.title: Best fits: 10 college basketball transfers who are perfect for new teams
articles.original_title: Best fits: 10 college basketball transfers who are perfect for new teams
articles.summary: Yahoo Sports highlights ten men’s college basketball players who have transferred to new programs and are considered ideal fits. The list examines how each player’s skill set aligns with the needs and style of their new school.

Analysis focuses on playing style, experience, and potential impact.
The article serves as a guide for fans tracking how transfers could reshape college lineups.

articles.title: Warriors wake up the echoes of their championship past in vintage comeback win over Clippers
articles.original_title: Warriors wake up the echoes of their championship past in vintage comeback win over Clippers
articles.summary: Stephen Curry erupted for 35 points, including seven 3‑pointers, as the Golden State Warriors rallied in the fourth quarter of an elimination game. Draymond Green provided a defensive stand by containing Kawhi Leonard, helping the Warriors complete the comeback against the Los Angeles Clippers.
key_points: Curry’s 35 points and seven threes powered the fourth‑quarter surge.
Green’s defense limited Leonard, shifting momentum in Golden State’s favor.
The win kept the Warriors alive in the play‑in tournament.

articles.title: NBA Play-In Tournament winners and losers: How ageless wonders Stephen Curry, Al Horford rescued the Warriors
articles.original_title: NBA Play-In Tournament winners and losers: How ageless wonders Stephen Curry, Al Horford rescued the Warriors
articles.summary: CBS Sports recaps the outcomes of the play‑in tournament, noting that the Oklahoma City Thunder benefited from the Clippers’ early exit. Veteran leaders Stephen Curry and Al Horford were highlighted for their crucial roles in keeping Golden State’s season alive.
key_points: The Thunder gained an advantageous position after the Clippers were eliminated.
Curry and Horford’s experience proved decisive for the Warriors’ play‑in success.
The article contrasts teams that advanced with those whose seasons ended.

articles.title: NBA touts near doubling of television viewership
articles.original_title: NBA touts near doubling of television viewership
articles.summary: The NBA reports that 170 million U.S. viewers tuned in to games across its four primary broadcast platforms during the regular season, marking an 86 % increase from the previous year. The league attributes the growth to expanded media partnerships and heightened fan interest.
key_points: 170 million viewers represent an 86 % rise over last season.
Viewership spanned broadcast, cable, streaming, and digital platforms.
The surge underscores the NBA’s expanding audience reach.

articles.title: Vintage Curry takes over as Dubs extend season
articles.original_title: Vintage Curry takes over as Dubs extend season
articles.summary: In a play‑in tournament matchup, Stephen Curry led the Golden State Warriors in erasing a 13‑point fourth‑quarter deficit to defeat the Los Angeles Clippers. The comeback secured Golden State’s advancement and extended their postseason run.
key_points: Curry’s scoring surge eliminated a 13‑point hole in the final quarter.
The Warriors advanced in the play‑in tournament, keeping their season alive.
The victory highlighted Curry’s clutch performance in high‑stakes games.

articles.title: Maxey Leads Sixers Past Magic, Secures Playoff Spot
articles.original_title: Minus Embiid, Maxey propels Sixers into playoffs
articles.summary: Tyrese Maxey scored 31 points as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Orlando Magic 109-97, clinching a first‑round playoff series against the Boston Celtics. Joel Embiid watched from the bench, but his absence did not hinder the team's victory.
key_points: Maxey's 31 points were the offensive catalyst for the win.
Philadelphia locked up a playoff matchup with the Boston Celtics.
Joel Embiid was sidelined but the team performed without him.

articles.title: LaMelo Ball Fined $35K for Reckless Contact with Bam Adebayo
articles.original_title: Hornets' Ball fined $35K for 'reckless' Bam play
articles.summary: The NBA fined Charlotte Hornets guard LaMelo Ball $35,000 for making “unnecessary and reckless” contact with Miami Heat center Bam Adebayo during a play‑in game. Ball also received a separate $25,000 fine for using profanity in a post‑game television interview.
key_points: $35,000 fine for the on‑court contact with Bam Adebayo.
$25,000 fine for profanity during a TV interview.
Both fines were issued by the league following the play‑in matchup.

articles.title: Timberwolves Aim to Flip the Switch After Uneven Season
articles.original_title: Wolves ready to 'flip the switch' off uneven season
articles.summary: Despite a lackluster regular season, the Minnesota Timberwolves, led by Anthony Edwards, expressed confidence heading into the playoffs against the Denver Nuggets. The team emphasized that they are now fully focused and ready to compete.
key_points: Anthony Edwards highlighted the team’s renewed focus.
Timberwolves are entering the postseason after an inconsistent year.
First playoff series will be against the Denver Nuggets.

articles.title: Referee Ben Taylor Exits Warriors‑Clippers Play‑In Game Due to Injury
articles.original_title: NBA referee Taylor exits Warriors-Clips with injury
articles.summary: NBA official Ben Taylor left the play‑in contest between the Golden State Warriors and the Los Angeles Clippers after sustaining an injury. The game continued with a replacement official.
key_points: Ben Taylor was forced to leave the game because of injury.
The incident occurred during the play‑in tournament matchup.
A backup referee took over to finish the contest.

articles.summary: ESPN analyst Zach Kram has projected the outcomes of every NBA playoff series, from the opening round through the Finals, outlining which teams he expects to advance and ultimately compete for the championship in June.

The statement was highlighted by Lakers Nation.

key_points: A league rule or procedural tweak favored LeBron’s style of play.
Lakers leveraged the advantage to stun the Rockets.

articles.title: Luka Dončić Extends International Scoring Streak
articles.original_title: Luka Doncic Extends NBA Scoring Streak For International Players - Lakers Nation
articles.summary: Dallas Mavericks star Luka Dončić continued his run of games with at least 20 points, extending the longest scoring streak by an international player in NBA history. The achievement was noted by Lakers Nation, underscoring Dončić’s consistency. No direct Lakers involvement was mentioned.
key_points: Dončić recorded another 20‑plus point game.
The streak sets a new benchmark for players born outside the U.S.
Lakers Nation reported the milestone.

articles.original_title: Cleveland Cavaliers allow best LeBron James to shine in Game 3 - NBA
articles.summary: In Game 3 of the Eastern Conference series, the Cleveland Cavaliers failed to contain LeBron James, who delivered a dominant performance for the Lakers. His scoring and playmaking highlighted a gap in the Cavs’ defensive strategy. The recap appeared on NBA.com.
key_points: LeBron posted a high‑scoring, high‑efficiency line.
Cavaliers’ defense was unable to limit his impact.
The game was a pivotal moment in the series.

The assist contributed to the Mavericks’ offensive flow.
Lake Show Life highlighted the play as a testament to his skill set.
